skill.name: popup-cro
skill.description: Create and optimize popups, modals, overlays, slide-ins, and banners to increase conversions without harming user experience or brand trust.
skill.summary: Create and optimize popups, modals, overlays, slide-ins, and banners to increase conversions without harming user experience or brand trust.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 15 static findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged normal English words ('design', 'desktop', 'system') as security issues. This is a benign popup CRO skill providing marketing strategy advice with no cryptographic code, network requests, or system access.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner incorrectly flagged 'design', 'desktop', 'described' as weak crypto algorithms. This is a benign popup CRO skill with no cryptographic code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Static scanner pattern matching incorrectly triggered on common English words like 'design', 'desktop', 'described'. No cryptographic code exists in this skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance (FALSE POSITIVE)
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner incorrectly flagged popup UX terminology ('system', 'mobile blockers') as system reconnaissance. This is legitimate marketing content about popup design.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Static scanner triggered on words 'system', 'blockers', 'interstitials' in legitimate popup CRO context. No system reconnaissance behavior present.

content.value_statement: Create popups and modals that capture leads and drive conversions without annoying users or harming your brand. Get strategy, copy, triggers, and design rules from conversion rate experts.
actual_capabilities: Design high-converting popup strategies with clear goals and triggers
Optimize popup timing, placement, and frequency for maximum engagement
Write effective popup copy with headlines, CTAs, and value propositions
Ensure popup accessibility and compliance with Google guidelines
Create mobile-friendly popups that do not block content
Set up frequency capping and visitor targeting rules
limitations: Does not write actual code or implement popups on websites
Does not integrate with specific popup tools or platforms
Does not provide A/B testing implementation details
Does not analyze existing popup performance data

use_cases.title: Email List Growth
use_cases.description: Design exit-intent and scroll-triggered popups that offer lead magnets or discounts in exchange for email signups
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and small business owners looking to grow their email list

use_cases.title: E-commerce Conversion Recovery
use_cases.description: Create targeted popups for visitors who add items to cart but do not complete checkout
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce store owners wanting to reduce cart abandonment

use_cases.title: Professional Popup Strategy
use_cases.description: Get comprehensive popup recommendations including type, trigger, copy, and mobile behavior
use_cases.target_user: Conversion rate optimization specialists and digital marketers

prompt_templates.title: Basic Popup Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me optimize my popup that currently has a 2% conversion rate. It appears on my blog after 10 seconds and offers a newsletter signup. What improvements would you recommend for timing, copy, and design?
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an existing popup with known performance data

prompt_templates.title: Exit Intent Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an exit intent popup for an e-commerce store selling premium headphones. The goal is to recover abandoned carts with a 10% discount. Include the headline, subhead, CTA button text, and decline copy.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating a specific popup type for e-commerce recovery

prompt_templates.title: Mobile Popup Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the best practices for mobile popups? My team wants to increase mobile signups without violating Google guidelines or harming mobile user experience.
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimizing for mobile-specific constraints and guidelines

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Popup Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to run both a welcome popup for new visitors offering a guide, and an exit intent popup for returning visitors offering a discount. How should I set up targeting and frequency rules to avoid showing too many popups?

output_examples.input: Help me create an email capture popup for a SaaS pricing page
output_examples.output:
Recommended popup type: Slide-in panel

Trigger: After 30 seconds OR when user moves cursor toward close button (exit intent)

Targeting: New visitors only, exclude converted users

Copy:
- Headline: 'Get Our Pricing Guide Free'
- Subhead: 'Includes feature comparison and insider tips'
- CTA: 'Send Me the Guide'
- Decline: 'No thanks, I'll figure it out'

Design notes: Use bottom-right slide-in, keep form to email-only field

Mobile: Bottom sheet format, tap to expand

output_examples.input: What metrics should I track for my popup campaign?
output_examples.output:
Key metrics to track:

1. Impression rate - How often popup displays vs eligible sessions
2. Conversion rate - Percentage of popup views that convert
3. Close rate - How many users dismiss without engaging
4. Time to close - How quickly users dismiss (indicates relevance)
5. Engagement before dismiss - Did users interact before closing?

Benchmarks:
- Email popup: 2-5% conversion is good
- Exit intent: 3-10% is strong
- Click-triggered: 10%+ indicates high intent

best_practices: Always provide clear, immediate value in the first 3 seconds of viewing
Use single-purpose popups with one clear CTA to avoid decision paralysis
Implement frequency capping - never show more than one popup per session
Ensure popups are easily dismissible with visible X, click-outside, and ESC key support
anti_patterns: Showing popups immediately on page load before users can engage with content
Using generic CTA buttons like 'Submit' or 'Learn More' instead of specific action language
Blocking access to content with full-screen popups that cannot be easily closed
Stacking multiple popups that overlap and create confusing user experience

faq.question: What is the best trigger for email capture popups?
faq.answer: Scroll-based triggers at 25-50% scroll depth or exit intent on desktop work best for email capture. These indicate genuine engagement without interrupting the user too early.

faq.question: Do popups hurt SEO?
faq.answer: Full-screen mobile interstitials before content can hurt Google rankings. Use non-intrusive formats like slide-ins or bottom sheets on mobile, and ensure popups do not block access to main content.

faq.question: How often should I show popups to the same visitor?
faq.answer: Maximum once per session with a 7-30 day cooldown after dismissal. Respect users who have already converted and exclude them from future popup displays.

faq.question: What makes a popup CTA button effective?
faq.answer: Use first-person language like 'Get My Guide' instead of generic 'Submit'. Be specific about what the user receives and use action verbs that create urgency.

faq.question: Are exit intent popups effective on mobile?
faq.answer: Exit intent does not work the same on mobile since there is no cursor. Use scroll-based triggers or target users who spend more than 30 seconds on pricing or product pages instead.

faq.question: Should I use different popups for new vs returning visitors?
faq.answer: Yes, show welcome popups with lead magnets to new visitors and use exit intent or behavior-based triggers for returning visitors who have already seen your initial offers.
